name: libiterator-util-perl
title: libiterator-util-perl
summary: Useful functions for creating and manipulating iterator objects
description:
Useful functions for creating and manipulating iterator objects
Iterator::Util implements many useful functions for creating and manipulating
iterator objects.
.
An "iterator" is an object, represented as a code block that generates the
"next value" of a sequence, and generally implemented as a closure. For
further information, including a tutorial on using iterator objects, see the
Iterator documentation.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use libiterator-util-perl because Useful functions for creating and manipulating iterator objects.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use libiterator-util-perl as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair libiterator-util-perl with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `libiterator-util-perl --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
